items.front: 6-3-1: What traffic pair does this paragraph separate?
items.back: A departing aircraft from an arriving aircraft making an instrument approach to the SAME AIRPORT.

items.front: 6-3-1a: Terminal fix-based — takeoff direction differs by ≥45° from reciprocal of final approach course
items.back: Departure takes off BEFORE arrival leaves a fix inbound NOT LESS THAN 4 MILES from airport.

items.front: 6-3-1b: Terminal fix-based — takeoff direction does NOT differ by ≥45° from reciprocal
items.back: Departure must be ESTABLISHED on a course diverging by ≥45° from reciprocal of final approach course BEFORE arrival leaves fix inbound not less than 4 miles.

items.front: 6-3-1c: When do the time-based minima (d/e) apply instead of fix-based (a/b)?
items.back: When BOTH: (1) no appropriate fix precludes a or b, AND (2) airports where approach control service is NOT provided. Both conditions must be met.

items.front: 6-3-1d: Time-based — takeoff direction differs by ≥45° from reciprocal
items.back: Departure takes off 3 MINUTES before arrival is ESTIMATED AT AIRPORT.

items.front: 6-3-1e: Time-based — takeoff direction does NOT differ by ≥45° from reciprocal
items.back: Departure takes off so established on diverging course: 5 MINUTES before arrival estimated at airport OR before arrival starts PROCEDURE TURN.

items.front: 6-3-1a vs 6-3-1b: What is the key difference when takeoff direction does NOT differ by ≥45°?
items.back: Subpara b adds 'established on a course diverging by ≥45°' — the aircraft must not just take off but be established on the diverging course before the arrival leaves the fix.
